name: AgentQL semantic locator layer
maintenance_status: Actively maintained. Current official release notes list SDK version 1.19.0 with recent remote-browser, session-reuse, proxy, iframe, and security dependency work. The repository was updated in May 2026, although GitHub releases are not used; version history is maintained in the documentation.
license: The public Python/JavaScript SDK and examples repository is MIT licensed, which is permissive and compatible with ApplyPilot's AGPL-3.0 distribution. The hosted AgentQL API, models, and Tetra browser remain proprietary services governed by the April 21, 2026 Terms; the service grant is limited to internal business use and cannot be sublicensed or resold.
pricing_model: Starter is $0/month with 50 API calls/month included, then $0.02 per AgentQL API call, 10 calls/minute, 10 remote-browser hours included, then $0.12/browser-hour, and 5 concurrent remote sessions. Professional is $99/month with 10,000 calls included, then $0.015/call, 50 calls/minute, 500 remote-browser hours included, then $0.10/browser-hour, and 100 concurrent remote sessions. The free trial includes 300 API calls and one remote-browser hour. Enterprise is custom and advertises dedicated cloud and on-premises deployment.
marginal_cost_per_attempt: [uncertain] A narrow form-locator route can batch many controls into one `query_elements` call, then use another query after page transitions and one confirmation query. At 2-5 calls, Starter variable cost is about $0.04-$0.10 after free calls; fully utilized Professional subscription amortization is about $0.0198-$0.0495 at $99/10,000 calls, before overage. A five-minute paid remote session adds about $0.01 at $0.12/hour, but local ApplyPilot Chrome avoids that charge. Actual calls depend on form pages, retries, ambiguous fields, and confirmation strategy.
projected_cost_per_verified_apply: [uncertain] Use C_attempt = AgentQL query cost + escape_rate * existing_fallback_cost + browser/human/maintenance cost, then divide total route cost by positively verified submits. For illustration only, three Starter calls cost $0.06; with a 30% escape to ApplyPilot's historical $0.8599 average Claude apply-agent row, model-route cost is about $0.318 per attempt before other costs. Completion and escape rates are unmeasured, so no defensible all-in verified projection exists yet. Compare the canary against the later $1.8530 fleet all-in baseline and per-host route baselines.
billing_granularity: Each AgentQL query is one API call regardless of how many terms are batched into that query, subject to plan-included calls and per-minute rate limits. Remote browsers are billed by browser hour after included hours; public pricing does not state rounding interval, idle rounding, data egress, screenshot, proxy, storage, or failed-call credit policy. Local browser use avoids remote-browser time but not AgentQL query charges.
model_cost: The hosted model cost is bundled per AgentQL API call rather than exposed as tokens. Official architecture says AgentQL selects among public GPT-4, Llama, Gemini, and proprietary models based on task complexity. Fast and Standard modes have the same public per-call price but trade speed for accuracy. Enterprise on-premises model and token economics are custom.
infrastructure_cost: [uncertain] With ApplyPilot's existing local Playwright/Chrome session, incremental infrastructure is small: SDK CPU for HTML/accessibility-tree preparation, network transfer, and existing browser time. Tetra remote-browser cost is explicit but proxy and enterprise infrastructure details are not. No official CPU, memory, payload-size, or local power profile is published.
human_recovery_cost: [uncertain] AgentQL provides element lookup, not an OTP relay, account-recovery workflow, or human-takeover queue. Human cost should remain close to the existing ApplyPilot profile/auth lane for login, OTP, sensitive questions, and ambiguous outcomes. The semantic layer may lower locator repair work but does not by itself reduce owner-auth minutes.
adapter_maintenance_tco: [uncertain] Expected to be lower than per-site CSS-selector repair because one structured semantic query can span related forms, but queries still need hierarchy hints, disambiguation, regression fixtures, API/version monitoring, privacy review, and deterministic validation. Measure query edits and engineering minutes by host; no vendor or ApplyPilot TCO data exists.
verified_completion_rate: [uncertain] Unknown. AgentQL documents cross-site element lookup, authenticated pages, and form submission, and old examples include a Workable job page, but it publishes no ATS-stratified job-application completion rate with positive submission evidence. Generic self-healing and accuracy claims are not substitutes for ApplyPilot canaries.
failure_modes: Element terms can resolve to `None`, resolve to the wrong similar control, miss hidden or conditional required fields, or become stale after navigation. Additional failures include fast-mode accuracy loss, backend timeout or 500, accessibility-tree generation defects, iframe or Unicode edge cases, Playwright actionability timeout, page crash, API-key/rate-limit failure, network loss, and a semantic match to the wrong irreversible button. Release notes show prior fixes in several of these areas.
crash_recovery: [uncertain] The SDK returns request IDs for server errors, exposes the last query, response, and accessibility tree, and fixed historical page-crash hangs. It does not provide a durable workflow engine, transactional step resume, or job retry policy. ApplyPilot must retain lease state, Playwright tracing, pre/post-submit classification, safe re-query rules, and ambiguous-submit quarantine.
exactly_once_submit_safety: AgentQL is only a locator and does not click unless caller code invokes Playwright, which makes a strong separation possible. It has no native idempotency or duplicate-application protocol. ApplyPilot should permit semantic lookup and form fill, then require a deterministic complete-plan checkpoint and a separately authorized single submit click; post-click uncertainty must remain `crash_unconfirmed`.
verification_evidence_tier: [uncertain] AgentQL provides semantic Locators, structured query results, request IDs, last accessibility tree/query/response, and optional debug screenshots. The surrounding Playwright page can capture URL, DOM, network, screenshot, video, and trace. A separate semantic confirmation query is useful but not sufficient alone; prioritize ATS ID or success response, then known confirmation DOM/URL, then email, and never locator confidence or console text alone.
verification_latency: [uncertain] Fast mode is intended to reduce response time and Standard mode to improve accuracy, but no current p50/p95 query latency is published. Default query timeouts can be large, including a historical 300-second `query_elements` default. Measure API round trips, page-ready waits, actionability waits, and delayed email reconciliation separately.
headless_headed_parity: [uncertain] AgentQL wraps Playwright pages in headed or headless local browsers and can attach to existing Chrome or use a remote Chrome session. No ATS-stratified parity benchmark is published. Because input is HTML plus accessibility tree rather than pixels, semantic matching may be less display-dependent, but page behavior and challenge handling can still differ.
python_integration: Strong. The maintained Python SDK wraps synchronous or asynchronous Playwright `Page` objects and supports Python >=3.9, compatible with ApplyPilot's Python >=3.11 runtime. Returned values are normal Playwright Locators, so existing fill, select, upload, wait, network, trace, and confirmation code can remain in Python.
playwright_cdp_reuse: Strong. Official docs show `wrap`/`wrap_async` over an existing Playwright page, Chrome CDP attachment to an open browser for preserved cookies/session state, Playwright `storage_state` reuse, and remote CDP sessions. This is a direct fit for ApplyPilot's existing Chrome profiles, CDP ports, Playwright fixtures, and deterministic final-submit code.
migration_effort: [uncertain] Medium for a narrow semantic-locator adapter and large if allowed to replace orchestration. Add an AgentQL client/config, fixed query schemas per form phase, local Page wrapping, required-field reconciliation, query-result logging, privacy controls, API cost accounting, fallback reasons, and confirmation queries. Keep queue, browser lifecycle, answerer, and submit guard unchanged.
ats_fit: [uncertain] Good conceptual fit for Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, Workable, and similar labeled forms because semantic queries can return multiple Playwright Locators across structurally different pages. Workday tenant flows, custom widgets, shadow DOM, nested iframes, and dynamically revealed questions require benchmarking. Stable high-volume ATS paths should still prefer deterministic adapters when their selectors are reliable.
deterministic_coverage: [uncertain] Locator selection is model-assisted on each uncached query, but actions after resolution are ordinary deterministic Playwright calls. A local validated selector cache could bypass AgentQL on warm forms, with AgentQL used only after fixed-selector failure. The share of ApplyPilot forms safely covered without a general browser agent is unknown until shadow testing.
escape_rate: [uncertain] Unknown. Escapes should include `None` terms, ambiguous or duplicate matches, unmapped required fields, unsupported controls, auth/challenge state, sensitive or unknown questions, API failure, and missing positive confirmation. Record semantic-to-deterministic, semantic-to-premium-agent, and semantic-to-human escapes separately.
control_plane_overhead: [uncertain] Lower than a turn-by-turn general browser agent when one query batches all controls: local code builds a simplified HTML/accessibility representation, sends it with a fixed query to AgentQL, receives element mappings, and performs actions locally. It still transmits page context and incurs one hosted model round trip per query. Payload bytes, context tokens, model choice, and p95 serialization overhead are not published.
observability_joinability: Strong with a small adapter. AgentQL exposes backend request IDs and last query/response/accessibility-tree data; debug logging and screenshots are available, while existing Playwright supplies trace, DOM, network, screenshot, and video. Persist ApplyPilot attempt ID, AgentQL request ID, query version/hash, mode, returned element metadata, action evidence, route, fallback reason, and confirmation tier in the same result event.
persistent_profiles: Local profile persistence uses normal Playwright mechanisms: save `storage_state` and load it into a new context, or attach over CDP to an existing Chrome profile. Tetra remote sessions can remain alive across disconnects until an inactivity timeout, but its `BrowserProfile` names such as LIGHT/STEALTH/TF_BROWSER describe runtime configuration, not documented durable authenticated storage snapshots. Keep durable candidate profiles under ApplyPilot control.
existing_session_attach: Strong. Official examples connect over CDP to an open Chrome browser, select its existing context, create or use a page, then wrap it with AgentQL. This preserves cookies and login state. ApplyPilot must keep its existing port ownership, profile lock, and one-account concurrency controls.
login_and_otp: AgentQL can operate behind authentication and documents login plus Playwright storage-state caching, but it does not provide a first-class OTP relay, password vault, or owner-takeover workflow. Reuse ApplyPilot's current profile, inbox/OTP, and supervised-auth lanes; AgentQL should only locate controls after authorization state is established.
extension_support: [uncertain] AgentQL offers a separate Chrome Debugger extension for query development, and CDP attachment can reuse a Chrome context that already has approved extensions. The SDK does not document extension installation, native messaging, or per-extension permission isolation. Use dedicated profiles and do not require the debugger extension in production.
session_data_boundary: Critical review item. Official architecture says AgentQL uses page HTML and accessibility trees and invokes public GPT-4, Llama, Gemini, and proprietary models; therefore page text and form context leave the local browser for hosted queries. Terms permit Tiny Fish to host, copy, transmit, process, and use Customer Data to provide, secure, maintain, and improve the service, plus retain aggregated/de-identified data. Tetra additionally hosts the browser. Cookies need not be sent explicitly, but rendered page content can contain candidate PII or filled values; query before injecting secrets where possible and require contractual privacy review for production.
required_field_completeness: [uncertain] A query can request a structured hierarchy of fields and returns `None` for unmatched terms, but automation mode assumes a one-to-one query-term-to-element mapping. It does not guarantee discovery of every visible, hidden, conditional, or client-validated required control. ApplyPilot must independently enumerate required DOM controls, reveal conditional fields, compare counts/labels to the answer plan, and fail closed on any mismatch.
answer_provenance: Strong if scope remains locator-only. AgentQL need not generate any answer; ApplyPilot can resolve all values through its resume/profile/approved-answer evidence pipeline and use returned Locators only for placement. Do not send candidate free-text generation to AgentQL or let semantic field descriptions authorize inferred answers.
unsupported_question_fallback: Define deterministic behavior: if any term is missing, ambiguous, sensitive, unknown, or not present in the approved answer plan, do not query more broadly and do not submit. Record the exact label/context and route to fixed selector repair, approved-answer lookup, premium agent, or owner review. This policy is caller-owned, not built into AgentQL.
prompt_injection_resistance: [uncertain] Fixed AgentQL query syntax narrows the instruction surface, but hosted LLMs still process hostile page text. No official documentation reviewed provides a page prompt-injection threat model, domain allowlist, secret non-disclosure guarantee for page content, or adversarial browser benchmark. Keep navigation domain-locked, query before filling secrets, restrict tools to locator retrieval, validate returned elements against form ancestry/type/label, and keep final submit deterministic.
site_permission_and_terms: AgentQL's April 2026 Terms require lawful use, permit only internal business use, prohibit interference with security features, and require use of Tiny Fish programmatic interfaces. They do not grant permission to automate third-party ATS sites. ApplyPilot must separately honor each ATS/employer terms and access controls, use reasonable rates, and route challenge pages to supervised/denied states rather than bypass them.
irreversible_action_guard: Strong architectural fit because AgentQL returns a Locator and Playwright performs the click. Keep the submit locator lookup separate from execution, verify it is inside the expected form and has the expected role/name, require complete approved answers and a durable submit intent, click once, then capture network/DOM confirmation. Never expose a generic `get_by_prompt('submit')` action directly to an autonomous loop.
concurrency: Starter allows 10 calls/minute and 5 concurrent Tetra sessions; Professional allows 50 calls/minute and 100 concurrent sessions. Local-browser concurrency is bounded by ApplyPilot workers/profiles rather than AgentQL session caps, but API calls remain rate-limited. Per-account ATS sessions should remain single-concurrency even if the vendor allows more.
startup_latency: [uncertain] Wrapping an already-open local Playwright page should add little local startup work, while each semantic query adds network/model latency. Tetra requires remote-session allocation and CDP connection. No p50/p95 allocation or query-start latency is published, so benchmark warm local CDP, cold local Chrome, and Tetra separately.
warm_session_reuse: Strong. ApplyPilot can keep the existing Chrome/CDP session and wrapped page, reuse Playwright storage state, or reconnect to a Tetra session configured to survive disconnects until inactivity timeout. Re-query after material DOM changes; do not reuse stale Locators across navigation.
tracing_and_replay: Good tracing through composition. AgentQL exposes request ID, query, response, accessibility tree, debug logs, and screenshots; Playwright adds trace, console, network, DOM, screenshots, and video. AgentQL does not provide deterministic semantic replay, so store query versions and resolved element evidence, and implement a local validated-selector cache for no-API replay experiments.
deployment_modes: Local Python or JavaScript SDK plus hosted AgentQL API, direct REST API, AgentQL-hosted Tetra Chrome, and custom Enterprise deployment are listed. The pricing page advertises Enterprise on-premises deployment. For initial ApplyPilot use, local Chrome plus hosted query API minimizes browser migration but has the broadest candidate-page data boundary.
browser_version_matrix: [uncertain] The SDK wraps Playwright Page objects and official examples use Chromium/Chrome, headed/headless, local CDP, and remote Chrome. Current docs do not publish a tested Chromium, Firefox, WebKit, Edge, CDP-version, or WebDriver BiDi matrix for semantic queries. Plan around current ApplyPilot Chrome/Chromium over CDP until cross-browser fixtures pass.
canary_stop_loss: AgentQL offers per-call billing, rate limits, API timeouts, and remote-session inactivity shutdown, but no ApplyPilot route circuit breaker. Enforce a maximum of five semantic calls per attempt, a wall-clock timeout, one Standard-mode retry only before form touch, no retry after submit intent, daily dollar cap, host failure-rate breaker, and immediate quarantine on ambiguous post-click state.
benchmark_design: Use a matched shadow set of at least 600 forms stratified by Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, Workable, Workday tenant, and long tail. Compare fixed selectors, AgentQL Fast, AgentQL Standard, and the current premium agent on identical pre-submit snapshots where possible. Measure required-control recall, correct-locator precision, answer-placement accuracy, query count, API/browser cost, latency, fallback, and confirmation evidence. After shadow success, canary at most 20 guarded submits per host class with no generic autonomous submit prompt.
route_funnel: Record preflight eligible -> deterministic selector/cache hit -> AgentQL selected -> query succeeded -> all required fields mapped -> answers provenance-approved -> fields filled -> local validation passed -> submit authorized -> submit locator validated -> one click -> synchronous confirmation -> delayed email confirmation -> verified applied, fallback, failed, blocked, or crash_unconfirmed. Preserve AgentQL request IDs and each fallback reason.
step_context_budget: Budget at most five AgentQL calls per attempt: one form inventory query, up to two conditional-page queries, one submit-control query, and one post-submit confirmation query. Batch related terms into each query, use Fast first and Standard only for a pre-submit ambiguous result, and record HTML/accessibility payload bytes, query terms, mode, latency, and returned matches. This budget is a canary guard, not evidence of sufficiency.
cache_replay_yield: [uncertain] AgentQL does not document a production selector replay cache. ApplyPilot can store a resolved selector fingerprint by ATS/tenant/form version, validate it cheaply on later pages, and call AgentQL only on cache miss. Measure warm hit, invalidation, wrong-match refusal, and no-API completion; do not cache submit controls without strict form and confirmation checks.
confidence_intervals: [uncertain] No matched data exists yet. Report Wilson 95% intervals for required-field recall, correct-locator precision, verified completion, fallback, and ambiguous/duplicate submit rates. Bootstrap cost per verified apply and latency. Promotion requires the lower bound on field recall and completion to meet the current route and the upper bound on irreversible-action error to remain no worse.
historical_bucket_impact: [uncertain] ApplyPilot history reports 44.7% of non-applied failures in agent/browser runtime and the later fleet snapshot at $1.8530 all-in per verified apply. AgentQL can reduce model turns and selector repair inside the variable-form slice, but it will not eliminate the 40.8% preflight/policy bucket or auth/challenge work. Savings equal batched query cost plus reduced premium-agent exposure, offset by semantic misses and fallback; route-level volumes and escape rates are not yet measured.
recommendation: CANARY as the preferred semantic bridge between deterministic selectors and a full browser agent. Keep the existing Playwright browser, profiles, answerer, queue, traces, and submit guard; use AgentQL only to return structured Locators. Start with shadow form inventory and fill, add a validated local selector cache, cap calls, and require positive confirmation. Production use needs candidate-data/privacy review because page HTML and accessibility content are processed by hosted models.
